paper_title: Constraints to Sustained and Inclusive Growth in Sri Lanka: An Initial Diagnosis and Key Topics for Discussion
research_answer: Sri Lanka's export basket has barely changed since the mid-1990s. In both 1995 and 2014, the country's exports were dominated by the same products: garments (women's undergarments, t-shirts, suits, sweaters), tea, and rubber products. As of 2014, Sri Lanka exported $11.1B total, with tea alone accounting for 12% of exports, women's undergarments 5%, and t-shirts 4%. This roughly two-decade stagnation in export composition is a striking sign of failed diversification — the country has not meaningfully moved up the value chain or into new product categories despite significant time passing.
supporting_quotes: Sri Lanka's export basket has changed little since mid-1990s... [comparing 1995 and 2014 exports showing dominance of Women's undergarments, T-shirts, Tea at both periods]
Exports, 2014... $11.1B USD total... [treemap showing Women's undergarments 5%, Tea 12%, T-shirts 4%, Women's suits 4%, Sweaters/pullovers 3%, Gloves/mittens 2%, etc.]
